feed.title: SAP Community - SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central
feed.subtitle: SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Update Parent fields depending on child fields forma MDF Object
entry.content:
Good afternoon!

We have two MDF objects with a Parent-Child relation. We need to modify a field of the parent object depending on the values ​​assigned in the child object at the time of saving the data.

However, when generating a rule with a base object “MDF Child” we cannot see the fields of the parent in the then clause.

If we generate a rule with a base object “MDF Parent” it is not executed at the time of saving if the fields of the parent object are not modified.

Is there a way to carry out this process through rules?

Can the permission to edit the parameters be assigned as with the Basic rules?

entry.title: Not able to find certification exam for C_THR81_2405
entry.content:
Not able to find certification exam for C_THR81_2405 under learning.sap.com>get certifications

Also, selecting any certification by clicking on 'Schedule Exam' is redirecting me "Buy Learning Hub" which I did not used to see earlier.

entry.title: Hide Payment End date for some pay component and for some company code
entry.content:
Hi All,

We want to hide "payment end date (Define end date)" for some pay component and some company code in compensation portlet. There are 3 pay components with "End-Dated Payments" = Yes, and many countries use them.
We want to hide the "payment end date " field for the only two company codes.
We tried to create a rule, but it doesn't work properly. 

So, could you please suggest is it possible to hide "payment end date" for some company code and some pay components using business rule or any other solutions? PFA screenshot for reference

Thank you

entry.title: Intelligent Service Center with Global Assignment
entry.content:
If we want to use Intelligent Service Center to trigger an integration when an event : 

- add Global Assignment

- End Global Assignment

is done by HR.

 

We need to enable the standard prepackaged business rules : 

SAP_EVENT_ENDGA

SAP_EVENT_ADDGA

these prepackaged business rule are using entity Jobinformation 

(cf https://help.sap.com/doc/e3eafa2654d84564a039e8bcb6218ea0/2411/en-US/SF_IS_SettingUp_Impl.pdf)

there is no details where we need to add the business rule.

we have added a post-save business rule in job information under Manager Business Configuration but the number of Global Assignments in Intelligent Service Center > Add Global Assignment remains to zero

even after creating a new Global assignment

 

we don't know if the problem is on the business rules or on manage business configuration.

if we need to add the business rule elsewhere, please let us know.

entry.title: HRIS Sync Mapping Guidelines
entry.content:
Dear Colleagues,

In HRIS Sync, is it acceptable to map multiple standard fields to a single HRIS field? So that a single HRIS field data is transmitted to multiple standard fields. In the below example, considering the emplStatus is already hardcoded, can we have below custom mapping to a custom field. I think yes.

Also are there any limitations that prevent mapping one standard element and one user info element together? For example, below: 

I have referred to a few SAP notes and help documentation, but the statements are often contradicting.

entry.title: Approval Workflows - Report on approval timestamp
entry.content:
Hi,

to comply with auditing authorities our customer asked to have a report that shows the approval timestamp for each step of each approval workflows.

We've seen that the there is no way to display the full timestamp on the workflow details page.

Also, the diagnostic tool store the data only for 10 days after the last action on the WF.

We've also tried to create a canvas report with domain Workflow WF Request Step but also here the "Last Modified Date" field only provides the date and not the timestamp.

Did anyone ever received such a requirement and found a way to fulfill it?

Every suggestion is highly appreciated.

Have a great day ahead averyone, and thanks in advance!

entry.title: Recruit-to-Hire Data Mapping for nonrecurring pay components
entry.content:
Hello,

I am seeking recommendations and best practices on the following issue. We are seeing nonrecurring pay components with a value of $0 coming over from the RCM Offer Approval into Onboarding as they are mapped in Recruit-to-Hire Data Mapping. The business would have to manually delete the $0 pay components in Onboarding before submitting.

RCM Offer Approval:

Recruit-to-Hire Data Mapping:

Onboarding:

Is there a way to prevent $0 nonrecurring pay components from coming over into Onboarding so we can prevent the manual work involved in deleting those pay components before submitting?

The behavior I’m observing in the system is all-or-nothing: when they are mapped, everything, including the $0 pay components, is transferred over. If they are not mapped, then the business would have to manually add those pay components.

entry.title: Terminate employee with no access to SSFF
entry.content: it is possible if i have a terminate employee that the employee still have access to SSFF?

entry.title: Position Copy Permission - Recruiter
entry.content:
Hi Community,

One of my customers has this following ask. We have a Recruiter field in while creating position. The requirement here is that for each position, only the recruiter whose name is specified in the "Recruiter Name" field will have permission to edit that specific position.

For example, if the "Recruiter Name" for Position Code 1000 is mentioned as Rashmi, only Rashmi will have the permission to edit that position. PFA the screenshot.

Is it feasible to achieve, or would any workaround be really helpful?

Let me know if you have any questions or need further clarification.

Thank you.

entry.title: Feasibility of Workflow in Document Generation in SuccessFactors
entry.content:
Hi Team,

I hope you're doing well.

One of my customers has this following ask. Is it feasible to set up a workflow during the document generation process in SuccessFactors. Specifically, if a workflow can be triggered or configured while generating documents in employee profile. PFA the screenshot.

Is it feasible to achieve, or would any workaround be really helpful.

Thank you.

entry.title: Report on Changes made to RBP Permission Roles
entry.content:
Hi,

I have a requirement to report on all changes made to Permission Roles over a period of +- one year? I have tried to use the Change Audit Report > RBP Role Change Report in SuccessFactors. However, I get an error, "You have exceeded the maximum date range of 31 days".

Is it possible to report on to report on all changes made to Permission Roles over a period of time longer than 31 days?

Thanks

KLV

entry.title: EC Compensation information API to receive and display salary data
entry.content:
Dear community, 

we have SF-EC with integration to a third-party-payroll system - ECP is not in place. All salary data is maintained in that system. There is a requirement to replicate certain salary data from the third-party-payroll system into SF in order to display them in Compensation Information portlet. The Compensation Information standard API seems to only support "Get" operation what means (in my understanding) that data can only be received from SF but not replicated into. 

Is that correct and does someone know other opportunities to cover this requirement?

Thank you and kind regards, 

Daniel

entry.title: Calculate Benefit Allowance with Comp Info Integration
entry.content:
I have a benefit allowance that is prorated depending on the employee's eligibility date. However, succeeding allowance amounts should be paid in full after.

Example: Transportation allowance = 15,000 every quarter for all employees

Expectation:

  1. Employee becomes eligible in the middle of the month: July 15
  2. Pay comp recurring should show 12,500 for July quarter (Q3 July-Sept) with end date Sept 30
  3. Pay comp recurring should show 15,000 effective in Q4 Oct-Dec

Actual:

  1. Employee becomes eligible in the middle of the month: July 15
  2. Pay comp recurring shows 12,500 for July quarter (Q3 July-Sept)
  3. No end date for 12,500 July entry in pay comp recurring

My rule correctly computes for the prorated amount and displays this amount as of eligibility date in the benefits section and comp info section. However, succeeding full amounts are not shown correctly, hence employee is still going to receive 12,500 in the next quarter instead of the full amount. How can I display #3 correctly in the comp info portlet? I understand the new benefit job only picks up employees with changes in their profile but what if there's no changes?

entry.title: Forward propagation on job info via CPI
entry.content:
Hello Guys,
Currently we see an issue when job info gets updated by CPI and on a retro date meaning not the top of the stack record, the changes are not forward propagated to the top of the stack record on job info. 

Just wanted to know if there is a method to achieve forward propagation in job info when changes are done via CPI .

Thanks in advance ..

entry.title: I need to Change Date Format in report storie
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community, have any of you had the opportunity to convert a text date to datetime format? I have a report where I call a field that contains a date, but it gives me “04 Dec 2024” and I need to change it to dd/mm/yyyy format in order to allow me to perform calculations.

I would appreciate the support if anyone has had the experience.

entry.title: Time accounts
entry.content:
Hello,

There is a requirement which must be following rolling period concept. There are around 5 leave types which are under rolling period which will have cap of 480 hrs. Along with that each individual leave types have their own max number of hours.

Below is the example of leave type

Leave Type

Policy explanation

A

1. Max 48hrs per application

2. should be based on rolling period

3. When leave applied then it should get adjusted in Annual Leave and Sick Leave. Along with that it should be adjusted against 480hrs.

B

1. max 480hrs in total

2. Should follow the rolling period concept

C

1. max 160hrs per application

2. there should be two picklists, where one picklist with yes or no to agree the policy and the second picklist is sub leave type. 

3. Second picklist value to consider as 1,2,3

D

1. Similar to Leave type C where it will be 2 picklists.

2. One with paid or unpaid as options. Where paid will have 64hrs application max and unpaid will have max of 16hrs per application

E

1. Max 560hrs to be available which is beyond the mentioned cap hours

2. The limit for this leave is beyond the cap 480 hrs. However, it will run concurrently with the cap hours.

 

Below is the example for the Rolling period policy

R.P Start Date

3-Mar-25

R.P End Date

2-Mar-26

Max hrs is 480 including all leave types which has the rolling period concept

 

Rolling Year Leave types

Start Date

End Date

Calendar days

Hours

Calculation during rolling year
** Thumb Rule : Maximum of 12 Weeks/672 hours = FMLA Unpaid in a rolling year

Remarks

A

3-Mar-25

8-Mar-25

6

48

432

System should allow employee to apply leave as it is firs leave application and the rolling period starts from 03-March-2025, paralley Annual Leave and SL wll be deducted and if there is no sufficient balance combining both PTO and SL it should throw error


B

1-Apr-25

20-Apr-25

20

160

272

System should allow employee to apply leave, as this is within rolling period, no PTO accrual happens

D unpaid

30-Aug-26

31-Aug-26

10

16

No hours reduces in the current rolling period

System should consider the current leave appliaction dates as the another rolling period as the current rolling period ends on 02-March-2026.

C

12-Dec-25

16-Dec-25

5

40

232

System should allow employee to apply leave, as this is within rolling period,  ALaccrual happens

D paid

14-Jan-26

15-Jan-26

2

16

216

System should allow employee to apply leave, as this is within rolling period, ALaccrual happens

C

18-Dec-25

22-Dec-25

5

40

192

System should allow employee to apply leave, as this is within rolling period,AL accrual happens

D unpaid

14-Feb-26

15-Feb-26

10

16

176

System should allow employee to apply leave, as this is within rolling period, AL accrual happens

E

1-Mar-26

9-May-26

70

560

-344

1. In this Scenario, even if employee do not have enough  availability for the rolling year but since its 'E Leave', system will allow and admin team will take care of this application from Backend.

2. Another scenario can be, where E Leave is the first application in terms of rolling year then system should not allow any further A /B/C/D for that respective rolling year.

Based on the above scenario, when the last leave ‘E’ is applied the end date is beyond the End date of rolling period. In this scenario new rolling period will be started from 3rd-March-2026 again until 02-March-2027

entry.title: Automated currency conversion to local currencies and keeping them on employee profile
entry.content:
Dear all,

As a global company managing its operations centrally, some payments, such as compensation and global benefits, are specified in USD. However, payroll must be processed in local currencies according to local payroll legislations. Local currency amounts can change monthly based on currency rates.

Since system do not provide any automatic currency conversion, conversion to local currency should be managed and uploaded to employee profile manually for every month. Manuel conversion is prone to human error.

We are asking for a solution. I submitted an improvement request. I am kindly asking for your votes.

Here is the link: https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/idea/336389

Request ID: 336389

Thanks in advance for your support.

entry.title: Identify the workflow and the business rule that fired it
entry.content:
I begin with Workflow Request.

I wish to work backwards to find:
a. The workflow that was fired.
b. the business rule that fired the workflow.

TIA,

sandy

I wis

entry.title: Contingent Worker ID Generation does not work as before
entry.content:
Dear Community,

Contingent Worker ID Generation does not work as before, Emp ID with CW prefix was generated. Now only a number is generated.

After 1H2024 Release, it was possible to opting out of the feature "Enable Centralized Services to Add Contingent Worker and Add Assignment" in Company System and Logo Settings, the Employee ID of Contingent Workers was generated as per the business rule again.

Now it is not possible to opt out "Enable Centralized Services to Add Contingent Worker and Add Assignment" feature. Currently User ID Generation for Contingent Worker follows the "Next Employee ID Assigned" in Company System and Logo Settings. However it does not support alphanumeric values having the CW prefix. 

Do you have the same issue? Have you found any solution to fix this problem? I opened a SAP support ticket and the agent said that there is no solution for this issue. But maybe there is a special workaround what can help to keep the original settings and continue using CW prefix in the Employee ID of the CWs.

Thanks.
Regards,
Szabi

entry.title: ONB -> EC MDF Object Integration, what happens when EC MDF object has a parent-child relationship?
entry.content:
Hi everyone!

After following some blogs from the community, trying to collect data with MDF Objects in the Additional Data Collection Step in Onboarding and pass it to the MDF Objects from EC, we realized that when the Employee Central MDF Object has a parent-child relationship, the solution provided in these blogs doesn´t work. 

Does anyone know if it is also possible to use the Integration Center when the objects in EC have this kind of relationship or the solution provided by these blogs is just applicable when the destination MDF in EC are individual. If it is possible then, what are we missing?

If not, what could we do to workaround this limitation?

Here are the blogs mentioned in this post:

 ONB-EC Integration: Sending data from Custom Data ... - SAP Community

Collecting Vaccine Data from Onboardee with SAP Su... - SAP Community

 

Thank you so much for sharing you´re knowledge. 

 

SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central - Employee Profile SAP SuccessFactors Onboarding OData SAP SuccessFactors HCM Core
